title: Finger injury follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

treatment_and_management.text: Supports selected orthopaedic follow-up, stability review, weight-bearing, cast or fixation context, and return review for non-improving symptoms in specified fracture populations.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports ongoing non-complex fracture management review for finger-injury follow-up; no clinical decision is generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports selected orthopaedic follow-up, stability review, weight-bearing, cast or fixation context, and return review for non-improving symptoms in specified fracture populations.

treatment_and_management.text: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports documented information and follow-up for finger-injury follow-up; no clinical decision is generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Original injury, diagnosis, alignment, wound, pain, swelling, motion, tendon function, sensation, perfusion, imaging and treatment are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for finger-injury follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
